title: Straight refactor-philosophy: one consistent style across a 3-function module
prompt:
Given this module:

```
defmodule MyApp.StatusService do
  def label(status) do
    case status do
      :active -> "Active"
      :pending -> "Pending"
      _ -> "Unknown"
    end
  end

  def color(status) do
    if status == :active do
      "green"
    else
      if status == :pending do
        "yellow"
      else
        "gray"
      end
    end
  end

  def icon(:active), do: "checkmark"
  def icon(:pending), do: "clock"
  def icon(_), do: "question"
end
```

Refactor so all three functions use the same style: multi-clause function heads matching on the literal atoms. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.StatusService do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Consistent-style refactor across three functions; only one canonical target (function heads). Easy because all three transforms are independent and obvious.
